title: Faulty Rotated Binary Search with Edge Cases

statement.markdown: Given a rotated sorted array nums of distinct integers, find the minimum using binary search. However, the array can also be empty and the function should return 0 in such cases. Additionally, the function should not modify the input array.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a list of integers and an optional boolean flag indicating if the array is sorted.
statement.outputFormat: Return the index of the minimum element in the array, or -1 if the array is empty.
statement.notes: Note that the array can have negative numbers.

hints.text: Consider handling the edge case where the array is empty separately
Use binary search to find the minimum element in the array
Ensure the function does not modify the input array

editorial.title: Faulty Rotated Binary Search with Edge Cases
editorial.intuition: Binary search can be modified to find the minimum element in a rotated sorted array
editorial.bruteForce: Check every element in the array to find the minimum
editorial.betterSolution: Use a modified version of the binary search algorithm to find the minimum element
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a single binary search pass to find the minimum element and handle edge cases
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Show that the modified binary search algorithm finds the minimum element in O(log n) time
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run of the corrected code with inputs and expected outputs

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int findMin(vector<int>& nums); }; class Solution { public: int findMin(vector<int>& nums) { if (nums.size() == 0) return 0; // TODO: implement } }
starterCode.java: class Solution { public int findMin(int[] nums); }; class Solution { public int findMin(int[] nums) { if (nums.length == 0) return 0; // TODO: implement } }
starterCode.python: class Solution(object): def findMin(self, nums): pass # TODO: implement

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int findMin(vector<int>& nums) { int low = 0, high = nums.size() - 1; while (low <= high) { int mid = low + (high - low) / 2; if (mid > nums.size() - 1 || mid < 0) continue; if (nums[mid] == nums[nums.size() - 1]) return nums.size() - 1; if (nums[mid] > nums[0]) low = mid + 1; else high = mid - 1; } return nums[0]; } }
buggyStarterCode.java: class Solution { public int findMin(int[] nums) { int low = 0, high = nums.length - 1; while (low <= high) { int mid = low + (high - low) / 2; if (mid > nums.length - 1 || mid < 0) continue; if (nums[mid] == nums[nums.length - 1]) return nums.length - 1; if (nums[mid] > nums[0]) low = mid + 1; else high = mid - 1; } return nums[0]; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution(object): def findMin(self, nums): low, high = 0, len(nums) - 1 while low <= high: mid = low + (high - low) / 2 if mid > len(nums) - 1 or mid < 0: continue if nums[mid] == nums[-1]: return len(nums) - 1 if nums[mid] > nums[0]: low = mid + 1 else: high = mid - 1 return nums[0]
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function findMin(nums) { var low = 0, high = nums.length - 1; while (low <= high) { var mid = low + (high - low) / 2; if (mid > nums.length - 1 || mid < 0) continue; if (nums[mid] == nums[nums.length - 1]) return nums.length - 1; if (nums[mid] > nums[0]) low = mid + 1; else high = mid - 1; } return nums[0]; }
